meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Master SaaS Development for Students
content.seo_description: Overcome the lack of SaaS knowledge with this expert-led checklist. Build your first project using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your career.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the 'no SaaS knowledge' barrier requires moving beyond tutorials to a modular starter kit. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you can bypass months of boilerplate friction, launching a production-ready MVP that demonstrates actual engineering competency to potential employers.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank code editor, the weight of 'not knowing how to build a SaaS' pressing down on you. You've followed a dozen tutorials, yet the gap between a completed video and a unique, deployed product feels like a canyon. You aren't lacking talent; you are lacking a battle-tested framework.

Today, the paradigm shifts. We are stripping away the complexity of modern stack integration by utilizing a professional-grade SaaS starter kit. By combining the architectural precision of Antigravity with the autonomous coding speed of Claude Code, you will transform from a student into a builder who ships real value.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Schema
steps.description: Define your database models and authentication flow before writing a single line of application logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust Supabase-compatible schema for a SaaS platform including User profiles, Subscription tiers, and Project entities with Row Level Security policies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize database schema based on the Antigravity architecture file'

steps.title: Boilerplate Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the directory structure and middleware for your Next.js environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a production-ready Next.js 14+ boilerplate with Tailwind CSS, Shadcn UI, and modular folder structure for server actions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Scaffold the project structure using the generated Antigravity boilerplate'

steps.title: Implementing Auth & Payments
steps.description: Integrate Stripe and Clerk into your starter kit to handle the heavy lifting of user management and billing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide the configuration code for integrating Clerk authentication and Stripe webhook handlers for tiered monthly subscriptions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Integrate Clerk and Stripe webhooks into the auth middleware'

steps.title: Developing Feature Modules
steps.description: Build your specific value-add feature as an isolated module to maintain code cleanliness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean server-side action to process user data and return it to the dashboard component with proper error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement the core feature module using the established patterns'

steps.title: Testing and Deployment
steps.description: Run automated checks and prepare your application for a Vercel production deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CI/CD workflow script and a suite of E2E tests for the primary user flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure Vercel deployment settings and run E2E test suite'

pro_tips: Always build your database schema first; code is transient, but data structures define the longevity of your SaaS.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write; future-you will thank you when debugging complex middleware.
Prioritize 'Server Actions' over complex API routes to keep your frontend and backend logic tightly coupled and type-safe.
Don't reinvent authentication; use established providers like Clerk to ensure security compliance from day one.
Deploy to production every 48 hours to catch environment-specific bugs before they become architectural debt.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, begin adding telemetry using tools like LogSnag to understand how users interact with your features. Use this data to iterate on your next release.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the roadmap to bypass the common traps of SaaS development. Stop tinkering and start building your legacy today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
